name: Server correctness under 10k requests per endpoint
aim: 10000 requests per endpoint × 10 endpoints: correct status codes and JSON response bodies throughout

threshold.rationale: Every request must return the exact documented response

on_exceed: Server returns wrong status codes or invalid JSON under load — dashboard receives malformed responses

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Zero tolerance — every request must return exactly the documented response shape
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Dashboard JavaScript fails to parse response → UI shows error state
answered_questions.how_metrics_were_measured: HTTP status code + JSON field check per response

name: Hub max client cap enforced at connection time
aim: With maxClients=5, the 6th connection must be rejected (HTTP 503)

threshold.rationale: Unbounded client acceptance leads to OOM under connection flood

on_exceed: Hub accepts unlimited WebSocket connections → attacker or misbehaving dashboard opens thousands of connections → OOM kill

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Hard cap prevents resource exhaustion under connection flood
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Each reconnecting dashboard tab creates a new connection → OOM kill
answered_questions.how_load_was_generated: Sequential connection attempts via dialWebSocket helper
answered_questions.how_metrics_were_measured: dialWebSocket returns error if server sends 503 instead of 101
